project.name: InfoScreen
project.purpose: A local kiosk dashboard for a Surface/Ubuntu display with schedule, market, weather, event stream, local events, photos, and runtime status.
project.secondary_host: macOS for Apple Calendar export only

document_contract.metadata_json: Root project requirements, constraints, plan, quality gates, and cleanup backlog.
document_contract.README.md: How to use, install, start, verify, and troubleshoot the project.
document_contract.docs/api-spec.md: HTTP endpoints, request/response behavior, and runtime JSON interactions.
document_contract.docs/design.md: Concrete system design contracts and verification gates.
document_contract.docs/questions.md: Open implementation questions, project risks, decisions, and pending resolutions.

hard_constraints: Runtime paths must be verified on the running Surface host before being changed.
Mac schedule-sync changes must remain independent from Surface frontend, crawler, systemd, and API changes.
The Mac checkout must not be required to switch to a Surface feature branch for schedule sync.
serve_infoscreen.py must not inject CSS, replace script URLs, or patch HTML as a normal solution.
Checked-in frontend CSS and JS must not be duplicated across surface/web/ and surface/web/assets/.
HTTP file logging must remain enabled unless replaced by a documented logging migration.
Destructive git cleanup on a deployed Surface host requires a runtime backup first.
Docs must not contain guessed paths; environment-specific paths require verification commands.
Runtime code must not silently fall back to fake data, old crawlers, or unverified sources.

target_repository_layout.deploy/: Systemd unit templates and install scripts only.
target_repository_layout.docs/: Only api-spec.md, design.md, and questions.md should remain under docs/.
target_repository_layout.mac/: macOS Apple Calendar export and schedule sync only.
target_repository_layout.scripts/: Developer, CI, status, and setup scripts.
target_repository_layout.surface/: Surface app implementation and runtime Python entrypoints.

active_features.name: HTTP dashboard server
active_features.status: implemented; cleanup required to enforce the HTTP server boundary contract

active_features.name: Calendar schedule board
active_features.status: implemented; runtime path must stay aligned with the verified Surface target

active_features.name: Market and weather refresh
active_features.status: implemented; runtime file paths and docs must remain consistent

active_features.name: Event/news stream
active_features.status: implemented; timer and logging verification required

active_features.name: Local official event discovery
active_features.status: implemented; output quality and frontend rendering require verification

cleanup_plan.description: Keep README.md, metadata.json, docs/api-spec.md, docs/design.md, and docs/questions.md as the active documentation set; remove other docs files.
Require runtime backup before reset, clean, branch replacement, or file deletion on Surface.
Move/keep checked-in CSS and JS only under surface/web/assets/css and surface/web/assets/js; remove duplicate root and surface/web-level assets after verification.
Remove CSS injection, script URL replacement, and HTML patching from serve_infoscreen.py after source files are cleaned.
Ensure infoscreen-http.service writes append logs to surface/.env/logs/http.log and http.err.log.
Keep Mac schedule sync and Surface /schedule.json serving aligned to the verified target ~/infoscreen/schedule.json unless a separate migration changes it.
Ignore runtime files, logs, schedule.json, .env, pycache, and pyc files in a dedicated cleanup commit.

acceptance_criteria: Schedule endpoint returns the verified Surface schedule file.
Local event extraction output is verified against real results and debug data.
Frontend layout uses one state owner per panel and canonical assets paths.
HTTP file logs are written under surface/.env/logs/.
docs/ contains only api-spec.md, design.md, and questions.md.
Runtime and generated files are ignored and not committed.
